ddls:
CREATE TABLE "cities" (
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each city */,
  "city_name" TEXT /* Name of the city */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude coordinate of the city */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude coordinate of the city */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the city is located */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or state where the city is located */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the city */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("city_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "urban_development" (
  "development_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each development initiative */,
  "city_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the city related to the development */,
  "development_number" INTEGER /* Development initiative number */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the development was initiated or completed */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Type of development (foreign key to development_types) */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the development */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the development */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("development_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_urban_development_city_id FOREIGN KEY ("city_id") REFERENCES cities ("city_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_urban_development_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES development_types ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for the user account */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., city planner, analyst, executive) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Password hash for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of the access event */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action performed (e.g., view, update) */,
  "resource" TEXT /* Resource accessed (e.g., city data, development project) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user's device */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "development_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for the type of development */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the development type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the development type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "project_details" (
  "project_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each project */,
  "development_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the urban development initiative it belongs to */,
  "project_name" TEXT /* Name of the project */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget allocated for the project */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the project (e.g., ongoing, completed, planned) */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the project */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the project */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("project_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_project_details_development_id FOREIGN KEY ("development_id") REFERENCES urban_development ("development_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "feedback_text" TEXT /* Text of the feedback */,
  "submitted_on" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was submitted */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating provided by the user (e.g., 1-5) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Content of the notification */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the notification was created */,
  "read" INTEGER /* Whether the notification has been read by the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id")
)
